---
title: Projection of tumour cells on developing hindbrain with SingleR
date: 2025-02-25
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

## Goal of this analysis

To determine the MB tumour's closest cell type using RL lineage cells as a reference. Same as the analysis from 2025-02-18 except that the updated UBC subclustering from Quang Trinh is used here instead.


## Files and directories

### Code

The code can be found in `HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/01_project_on_dev_ref.R`. All code was run using the `scrnaseq_env` conda environment.

Briefly, the script takes the following files as input:

- the Vladoiu scRNA-seq MB tumour dataset with 8 samples; the tumour cells will be annotated using the cell types in the reference dataset
- the reference datasets:
  - the integrated Aldinger + Sepp human RL lineage cells (performed by Shraddha)
  - the UBCs that were subset from the dataset above (performed by Quang)

See the notes from 2025-02-18 for the flowchart.

### Output

The output can be found in `<pailab projects>/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250225/`. The bash script that was used to call the R script can also be found in this folder.

Results are located in three output folders (`full_RL_lineage`, `full_RL_then_UBC`, `merged_RL_with_UBC`; see flowchart from 2025-02-18). Each folder contains:

- `prediction_results.rds`: output of `SingleR` with the score and assigned label for each cell in the tumour dataset
- `score_heatmap.png`: `plotScoreHeatmap` showing each tumour cell's score for each reference developmental cell type
- `annot_bar.png`: Bar plots showing the distribution of tumour cells
- `umaps.png`: UMAP of the tumour cells
- `delta_distr.png`: `plotDeltaDistribution` showing the delta score for each cell, i.e., the difference between the score for the assigned label and the median score of all other predictions
- `score.distr.png`: `plotScoreDistribution` showing the distribution of scores for each assigned label


## Results

### Using the RL lineage as a reference

The figures below show the results from using the general RL lineage as the reference (with UBCs as one cluster). The results here should be unchanged from the initial run on 2025-02-18.

![Heatmap of SingleR scores for each of the tumour cells. Annotations above the heatplot show the tumour cell's final assigned cell type and its tumour subtype.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250225/full_RL_lineage/score_heatmap.png){#fig-rl-only-heatmap}

![Proportion of MB tumour cells by subtype, sample, or cluster. Colours indicate the Seurat cluster (top) or the cell type annotation as predicted by SingleR (middle and bottom).](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250225/full_RL_lineage/annot_bar.png){#fig-rl-only-bar}

![UMAP of the tumour cells coloured by sample (left), subtype (middle), and SingleR-assigned cell type (right).](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250225/full_RL_lineage/umaps.png){#fig-rl-only-umap}

As expected, SHH tumours have a higher proportion of GN labels than Group 3/4 tumours which have higher proportions of RL and UBC labels (@fig-rl-only-bar, middle left). However, the SHH tumours still have a substantial number of cells which were labelled as RL and UBC.

### Splitting the UBC into clusters

The figures below show the results after taking the MB tumour cells that were assigned the UBC label and re-running SingleR with the UBC subclusters.

The UBC clustering was performed by Quang. There are fewer UBCs in this reference compared to the previous run as the clustering was limited to the cells that were *EOMES*+. The labels from the 0.5 clustering resolution were used as the reference (`SCT_snn_res.0.5`).

![Heatmap of SingleR scores for each of the tumour cells. Annotations above the heatplot show the tumour cell's final assigned cell type and its tumour subtype.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250225/full_RL_then_UBC/score_heatmap.png){#fig-rl-then-ubc-heatmap}

![Proportion of MB tumour cells by subtype, sample, or cluster. Colours indicate the Seurat cluster (top) or the cell type annotation as predicted by SingleR (middle and bottom).](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250225/full_RL_then_UBC/annot_bar.png){#fig-rl-then-ubc-bar}

![UMAP of the tumour cells coloured by sample (left), subtype (middle), and SingleR-assigned cell type (right).](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250225/full_RL_then_UBC/umaps.png){#fig-rl-then-ubc-umap}

### Merging RL lineage and UBC subclusters

The figures below show the results from combining the RL lineage and UBC subcluster labels to use as the reference, and running SingleR on the merged cell type annotations.

Note that a generic UBC label remains since not all cells that were labelled "UBC" were *EOMES*+. The *EOMES*- cells were not included in the UBC subclustering.

![Heatmap of SingleR scores for each of the tumour cells. Annotations above the heatplot show the tumour cell's final assigned cell type and its tumour subtype.](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250225/merged_RL_with_UBC/score_heatmap.png){#fig-merged-heatmap}

![Proportion of MB tumour cells by subtype, sample, or cluster. Colours indicate the Seurat cluster (top) or the cell type annotation as predicted by SingleR (middle and bottom).](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250225/merged_RL_with_UBC/annot_bar.png){#fig-merged-bar}

![UMAP of the tumour cells coloured by sample (left), subtype (middle), and SingleR-assigned cell type (right).](/.mounts/labs/pailab/private/projects/HumanMouseUBC/tumour_projection/SingleR/20250225/merged_RL_with_UBC/umaps.png){#fig-merged-umap}


## Summary and next steps

Looking at Figures [-@fig-merged-heatmap]-[-@fig-merged-umap], we see that the majority of the tumour cells were labelled as the UBC_2 subcluster or the generic UBCs. SHH tumours have a higher proportion of generic UBCs while Group 4 tumours have a higher proportion of UBC_2 (@fig-merged-bar, middle left). This is unexpected as SHH tumours have been shown to be most similar to the GCP/GN lineage. The non-neuronal lineages in clusters 8 and 11 appear to labelled properly (compare @fig-rl-only-bar and @fig-merged-bar, bottom row).

Given that nearly all cells are being labelled as UBC or UBC_2, the `full_RL_then_UBC` results (Figures [-@fig-rl-then-ubc-heatmap]-[-@fig-rl-then-ubc-umap]) may also be a result of this artifact; the cells were mostly labelled as UBC_2 (and some UBC_0) (@fig-rl-then-ubc-bar).

One potential reason for the dominance of UBC_2 could be a lack of distinct cluster markers. Instead, the markers that are being identified may be too generic, causing nearly all the cells to be annotated as UBC_2. Differential expression should be run to check if UBC_2 is lacking cluster-specific markers. Quang also suggested running `scDblFinder` to check if any of the clusters represent doublets.
